rationale: The digit-boundary fix (2b55d1a7) implements exactly the hardening my round-1 advisory suggested and I verified it empirically with fresh fixtures against the real ValidateSpec: [ADR-00311] with ADR-0031 present no longer false-passes via the 4-digit prefix (no match, no wrong-ID resolution), and [ADR-12345] no longer emits the misleading truncated ADR-1234 error — both directions of the defect are gone, with no new error class (parity preserved, correctly so: 5-digit ids are outside the spec's pinned ADR-XXXX shape). The boundary introduces no new blind spot: empty-tail bare [ADR-9999], filename-form [ADR-9999-x.md], non-digit-tail [ADR-9999X], space-tail, adjacent same-line links, angle-bracket destinations, and multiline link text all still reject when dangling; existing bare-ID and filename-form anchors still pass. All round-1 probes reproduce unchanged: ref-style/escaped-bracket misses (out of pinned shape), fence/comment/paragraph over-matches (zero real-corpus hits), byte-identical R5 parity, no coverage/relevance leakage. Corpus sweep unchanged at 11/111 (all R5, zero adr-touchpoint-missing), 110 self-check exits 0, internal/validate + internal/approve green.
findings: Round-1 advisory ADDRESSED in both directions: at 2b55d1a7, [ADR-00311](...) with ADR-0031 in the store produces no diagnostic and is never resolved against ADR-0031 (round 1: silent false pass via prefix capture); [ADR-12345](...) produces no diagnostic (round 1: SevError naming the never-written ADR-1234). Six-digit [ADR-003111] likewise falls outside the pattern.

The chosen semantics — 5-digit ids fall outside extraction entirely rather than becoming a new error class — is the parity-correct disposition: the spec's pinned extraction shape is the 4-digit ADR-XXXX, and promoting unparseable ids to errors would be stricter than plan-approve. The residual (a dangling 5-digit typo link is simply unchecked) is inherent to that pin and now honest: the check never claims to have verified an ADR the author didn't write.

No new blind spot from the boundary group ([^0-9\]][^\]]*)?: empty-tail bare [ADR-9999](...) still SevError when missing and passes when existing; filename-form [ADR-9999-x.md] still SevError when missing, [ADR-0031-doc-sync-gate.md] passes when existing; non-digit tails ([ADR-9999X], space tails) still extracted; two dangling links on one line both reported; angle-bracket destinations and multiline link text still caught.

All round-1 probes reproduce byte-identically at 2b55d1a7: R5 spec-vs-plan impacted-domains-resolve messages identical across all five fixture shapes; existence-only boundary intact (Proposed-status, non-intersecting-domains, no-citation, malformed-ADR-file fixtures emit zero adr-coverage-*/adr-cite-irrelevant); ref-style and escaped-bracket links remain unextracted (unchanged, out of pinned shape).

Real-corpus oracle unchanged by the fix: 11/111 specs fail the two new checks, all under R5 impacted-domains-resolve (each already rejected identically by main's validate plan), zero adr-touchpoint-missing — the boundary regex flips no real spec in either direction, and specs 085-094's filename-form anchors (hyphen after the 4th digit) still resolve.

Verification: 110 self-check with the 2b55d1a7-built binary exits 0; go test ./internal/validate (including the three new boundary subtests) and ./internal/approve both PASS; delta confined to internal/validate/spec.go regex+comment and spec_test.go; worktree clean at the pinned SHA.
